stories.headline: Etched Is Raising Two Rounds at Once, at $10B and $20B, and Neither Has Closed
stories.key_takeaway: Etched is negotiating two simultaneous venture rounds at $10B and $20B valuations - a 2x spread on the same pre-shipment inference chip company - and neither deal has closed.

pull_quote.text: We're making the biggest bet in AI. If transformers go away, we'll die. But if they stick around, we're the biggest company of all time.

stories.article_excerpt: Three weeks ago, Etched was a four-year-old chip startup that had just come out of stealth with $800 million raised.

stories.headline: OpenAI Closes the Largest Private Round in History: $122 Billion at an $852 Billion Valuation
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's $122 billion close at an $852 billion valuation is the largest private round ever, but roughly 90 percent of it came from just three corporate backers and a third of Amazon's share is contingent on an IPO or AGI.

pull_quote.text: It's so early right now in the AI space, and OpenAI is off to an amazing start. They're going to be one of the very big winners, we believe, long term.

stories.article_excerpt: The number that ended the debate about whether private markets could still fund artificial intelligence at scale is $122 billion.

stories.headline: Microsoft Frontier Company Puts $2.5 Billion and 6,000 Engineers Behind Enterprise AI Deployment
stories.key_takeaway: Microsoft's $2.5 billion Frontier Company is the largest entry yet in a four-way race - with AWS, OpenAI and Anthropic - to make embedded deployment engineering, not model quality, the durable moat in enterprise AI.

pull_quote.text: This goes beyond what has been labeled as Forward-Deployed Engineering, and will be the largest, most capable, outcome-driven engineering organization in the industry.

stories.article_excerpt: In seventy-two hours at the end of June, two of the largest technology companies on earth committed roughly $3.5 billion.

stories.headline: Amazon's Custom Chip Business Passed a $20 Billion Run Rate, and Its Biggest Customers Are Nvidia's
stories.key_takeaway: Amazon's in-house silicon unit has crossed a $20 billion run rate with over $225 billion in Trainium commitments from Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta and Uber, eroding Nvidia's pricing power from inside the hyperscalers rather than beating it on benchmarks.

pull_quote.text: As best as we can tell, our custom silicon business is now one of the top three datacenter chip businesses in the world.

stories.article_excerpt: Somewhere between the Prime Day promos and the warehouse robots, Amazon quietly became one of the three largest chipmakers.

stories.headline: Bezos Backs CuspAI at $2.6 Billion, Quadrupling a Two-Year-Old Materials-Discovery Startup
stories.key_takeaway: CuspAI's $450M Series B at a $2.6B valuation, backed by Bezos Expeditions, Kleiner Perkins and NEA, marks the moment AI-for-science moved from research demo to venture-scale industrial category.

pull_quote.text: What sets Max, Chad, and the team apart is that they design for materials that can actually be built, not just ones a model can dream up.

stories.article_excerpt: Nine months ago, CuspAI was a $520 million bet on an idea: that a search engine for molecules could work.

stories.headline: Huawei's Atlas 950 SuperPoD Chains 8,192 Ascend Chips and Claims 6.7x Nvidia's NVL144
stories.key_takeaway: Huawei's Atlas 950 SuperPoD beats Nvidia's NVL144 by brute scale-out - 56.8x more chips for 6.7x the compute - proving China can build frontier-class AI racks without US silicon, at an undisclosed per-watt penalty.

pull_quote.text: For reasons we all know, we don't have access to advanced process nodes, so we've decided to focus our efforts on making breakthroughs by combining chips - essentially connecting more computing resources.
pull_quote.role: Deputy Chairman and Rotating Chairman, Huawei

stories.article_excerpt: For ten months, Huawei's Atlas 950 SuperPoD existed only as a slide deck and a promise. On July 16, it appeared.

stories.headline: GLM-5.2 Holds the Open-Weight Crown, but Only Until Kimi K3's Weights Drop on July 27
stories.key_takeaway: GLM-5.2 is the strongest open-weight model available today for long-horizon agentic coding, but Kimi K3's July 27 weight release gives its lead a known expiry date - and both contenders are Chinese labs.

pull_quote.text: Competition and collaboration are what push all of us forward.
pull_quote.role: Head of Global Operations, Zhipu AI

data_points.label: AA Intelligence Index, top open weight

stories.article_excerpt: Every leaderboard has a champion. Almost none of them come with an expiration date printed on the box.

stories.headline: IQVIA Put 150 Agents Into Clinical Trial Operations, the Largest Agentic Deployment in Pharma Yet
stories.key_takeaway: IQVIA has deployed 150+ agents across clinical trial operations with 19 of the top 20 pharma companies using them, but the open question is whether non-deterministic agentic systems can satisfy GxP validation and FDA's credibility framework.

pull_quote.text: We also need the human in the loop, because it might give us a very good draft - say 70% of the way there, even with agentic.

stories.article_excerpt: The most consequential agentic AI rollout of the year is not in a coding IDE or a customer service queue.

stories.headline: Brussels Orders Google to Open Android to Rival AI Assistants and Share Its Search Data
stories.key_takeaway: The European Commission has for the first time treated the AI assistant layer as a regulated chokepoint, ordering Google to open eleven Android features to rival assistants by August 2027 and to sell anonymised search data on FRAND terms from January 2027.

pull_quote.text: Today's decisions risk undermining vital privacy and security guardrails for millions of Europeans.
pull_quote.role: President of Global Affairs, Google and Alphabet

stories.article_excerpt: For a decade of antitrust fights, the thing regulators tried to pry out of Google's grip was the browser.

stories.headline: A Bipartisan Senate Bill Would Give Americans a Property Right in Their Own Face
stories.key_takeaway: The Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously advanced the NO FAKES Act, creating a federal, inheritable digital replication right in every American's voice and likeness - backed by unions and platforms, but opposed by civil liberties groups who say its takedown system will become a censorship tool.

pull_quote.text: Everyone deserves the right to own and protect their voice and likeness from AI deepfakes, no matter if you're Taylor Swift or an 8th grader in Wilmington.

stories.article_excerpt: American law has never quite decided what you own about yourself. Your name and image are protected in some states.

stories.headline: ICML's Distinguished Paper Says Diffusion Language Models' Signature Advantage Is Actually a Trap
stories.key_takeaway: An ICML 2026 award paper from Tsinghua shows diffusion language models exploit arbitrary-order decoding to skip high-uncertainty logical tokens, collapsing solution diversity - and that simply forcing left-to-right order during RL training beats far more complex diffusion-specific methods.

pull_quote.text: The flexibility of arbitrary order serves as a mechanism for inference-time exploitation rather than reasoning exploration. By bypassing high-uncertainty tokens, the model trades the exploration of diverse reasoning paths for greedy optimization of local consistency.

data_points.label: HumanEval problems only AR order solves

stories.article_excerpt: For two years, the pitch for diffusion language models has been the same: they can generate tokens in any order.

stories.headline: The Other Diffusion Win at ICML Was Theoretical, and It Makes Every Sampler Cheaper
stories.key_takeaway: An ICML 2026 Outstanding Paper proves diffusion samplers can hit a target accuracy in polylogarithmically many denoising steps instead of polynomially many, using score queries alone - an exponential theoretical cut to the dominant cost of generative media inference.

pull_quote.text: We present algorithms for diffusion model sampling which obtain error in polylog steps, given access to accurate score estimates. This is an exponential improvement over all previous results.

stories.article_excerpt: Every image a diffusion model generates is the end of a countdown. Twenty steps, fifty steps, a thousand steps.

stories.headline: AI Is Cutting 20 to 30 Percent Off the Time It Takes to Make Drugs for Clinical Testing
stories.key_takeaway: Bristol Myers Squibb's chief research officer says AI has already cut 20-30% off the time to produce medicines for clinical testing - a checkable manufacturing claim, not a discovery one, and one that moves a start date rather than an approval date.

pull_quote.text: Maybe before we could do 10 and now we can do dozens.
pull_quote.role: Chief Research Officer, Bristol Myers Squibb

data_points.label: Cut in time to clinical supply
Perf per megawatt of new cluster

stories.article_excerpt: The most credible AI-in-pharma number of the week did not come from a discovery claim. It came from the loading dock.

stories.headline: Protein AI Moves Past the Static Snapshot and Starts Predicting How Molecules Actually Move
stories.key_takeaway: Protein AI is pivoting from predicting a single static structure to sampling the full conformational ensemble - BioEmu does it orders of magnitude cheaper than molecular dynamics and BindCraft designs binders at a 46% hit rate - but the field still lacks the ground truth to know how far these models generalize.

pull_quote.text: With highly accurate structure prediction, protein dynamics is the next frontier in discovery. BioEmu marks a significant step in this direction by enabling blazing-fast sampling of the free-energy landscape of proteins.

data_points.label: Fold switchers solved out of training set

stories.article_excerpt: A protein structure predicted by AlphaFold is a photograph. Biology is a film. The gap between the two matters.

stories.headline: New Jersey Starts Asking Investment Advisers What Their AI Is Actually Doing With Client Money

sources.name: NJ Office of the Attorney General

stories.key_takeaway: New Jersey has folded detailed AI questions into the annual examination that all state-registered investment advisers must complete - not a formal sweep, but a live demonstration that existing fiduciary and anti-fraud law is binding AI use faster than any AI-specific statute.

pull_quote.text: Artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies may create new opportunities in the investment marketplace, but they also present new risks that demand careful oversight.
pull_quote.role: Bureau Chief, New Jersey Bureau of Securities

stories.article_excerpt: There is no New Jersey AI statute for investment advisers. There is no rule proposal, no notice-and-comment docket.
